etymology.interpretation: åḟḞ(åḟḞ) (nÃỲ, "contrary") depicts something going against the natural direction, combined with æỲ (æỲ ) (qiÃ n, "yawn; lack"), which represents an open mouth indicating a sense of incompleteness or deficiency. Together, they convey the idea of lacking something essential, as if going against what should naturally be complete.
